4.2.1Frontmatter4.2.2Themes4.2.3Layouts
4.3Directives

Themes

Build your theme with tokens, not giant CSS files.

Define your palette once, call @mqs-essentials;, then only override what you really want.

The core idea

1) URL / fonts

Keep your @import url(...) at the top if needed.

2) Define palette

Use @mqs-palette with normalized tokens: primary, secondary, ternary, background, etc.

3) Generate essentials

@mqs-essentials; expands to the base Marque theme automatically.

4) Override manually

Put custom CSS below essentials for fine tuning.

Minimal MQS theme

css
@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Sans:wght@300;400;500&display=swap");

@mqs-palette {
  primary: #c85a2a;
  secondary: #2a5ac8;
  ternary: #2ac852;

  background: #f7f5f0;
  surface: #ffffff;
  surface-alt: #eeece7;
  text: #1a1916;
  muted: #6b6860;
  border: rgba(0,0,0,0.09);

  radius: 8px;
  max-width: 860px;

  font-sans: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif;
  font-serif: Georgia, serif;
  font-mono: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace;
}

@mqs-essentials;

What essentials generates

  • Theme variables in :root
  • Typography and links
  • Nav and footer skin
  • Row, card, button, callout, stat
  • Tabs, steps, hero, badge
  • Responsive defaults

Complete @mqs-palette keys

You can edit all these keys today:

  • Core: primary, secondary, ternary, background, surface, surface-alt, text, muted, border, radius, max-width, font-sans, font-serif, font-mono
  • Nav pack: nav-bg, nav-text, nav-border, nav-active-bg, nav-active-text
  • Code pack: code-bg, code-text, code-border, code-head-bg, code-head-text
  • Card pack: card-bg, card-border, card-radius, card-shadow
  • Callout pack: callout-info-bg, callout-info-border, callout-info-text, callout-warn-bg, callout-warn-border, callout-warn-text, callout-danger-bg, callout-danger-border, callout-danger-text, callout-ok-bg, callout-ok-border, callout-ok-text

Aliases accepted in palette:

  • bg → background
  • accent → primary
  • accent2 → secondary

Normalized style classes

  • .primary
  • .secondary
  • .ternary

Compatibility classes

Existing styles still work:

  • .accent (primary)
  • .accent2 / .blue (secondary)

Overrides layer

Anything after @mqs-essentials; is your custom layer.

css
@mqs-essentials;

.mq-card.primary {
  border-top-width: 4px;
}

.mq-main h1 {
  letter-spacing: -0.04em;
}

Quick flow

  1. Create theme file: add themes/my-theme/theme.mqs.
  2. Paste minimal config: start with palette + essentials.
  3. Activate theme in marque.toml: theme = "my-theme".
  4. Run marque serve . or marque build ..

If theme.mqs exists, Marque uses it first. Otherwise it falls back to theme.css.
